purpose: Expose missing inputs, weak claims, and hidden operational risk
Grade build readiness against explicit criteria
Produce audit receipts before high-risk public changes
Prevent unsupported certification, compliance, or capability claims

questions: Is every public claim verifiable?
Does the page avoid unsupported certification or procurement claims?
Are assumptions stated instead of hidden?
Does the page present one primary buyer action?
Is the CTA path working and aligned with buyer intent?
Does the copy reduce friction for MSP/vendor/procurement buyers?
Does the workflow create a durable operational record?
Are failure modes visible and safe?
Is there a rollback path?
Are secrets excluded from source control?
Are write paths constrained?
Are public assets approved before publishing?
Does the page have self-referencing canonical metadata?
Are title and Open Graph tags unique?
Does the page target the intended buyer query?
Is the page readable across mobile and desktop?
Are labels, forms, and states clear?
Are visual defects or contrast problems present?
Is there one clear source of truth?
Are duplicate paths synchronized?
Can future agents modify the system without causing drift?

riskFlags: set-aside or sole-source claim without verified status
form with no receipt or durable record
public copy that reveals internal strategy
